title: Response-Equivalence Quotient on `large_amount_stress`
purpose: Test whether oracle separation on the `large_amount_stress` family boundary on LC322 survives quotient collapse by solver-response equivalence. Quotient restricted to the 5 family-3 probes; battery restricted to 4 of the 11 C-5 perturbations.
research_question: RQ-C7: Does the `large_amount_stress` family boundary reflect solver-internal structure or probe construction geometry? Operationalized: does oracle separation on the `large_amount_stress` family survive quotient collapse by solver-response equivalence?

input_probe_set_P.note: All 5 family-3 probes have axis=magnitude and deformation_level=0. The probes vary in amount (50, 50, 100, 50, 1000) and pairing.

quotient_construction.equivalence_relation: p ~ p' iff for all s in S: R(s, p) = R(s, p')
quotient_construction.quotient_population_T_P: T_P = P / ~, the set of equivalence classes of family-3 probes under solver-response equivalence
quotient_construction.abstract_probe_inheritance: Each abstract probe t in T_P inherits probe_family = 'large_amount_stress' from the original probes in the class
quotient_construction.size_range: 1 <= |T_P| <= 5 (computed in Step 3; declared here as a range, not a specific value)

P0_unperturbed.description: Unperturbed LC322. Quotient on R(s, p) for s in S_full (30 solvers).

P1_accept_reject_ratio_shift.description: Re-label LC322 from 11 ACCEPT / 19 REJECT to 19 ACCEPT / 11 REJECT. The 11 solvers formerly labeled ACCEPT are relabeled REJECT; the 19 formerly labeled REJECT are relabeled ACCEPT.
P1_accept_reject_ratio_shift.quotient_unchanged_from_P0: Equivalence relation is on R(s, p), not on oracle labels. P1 inverts oracle labels but R(s, p) is unchanged. The quotient population T_P1 equals T_P0.
P1_accept_reject_ratio_shift.estimator_decisions_unchanged: B1 and C_genuine operate on R(s, p), not on oracle labels. P1 does not change estimator decisions.

P2a_solver_subsample_first_20.description: First 20 solvers of LC322 (indices 0..19 in sorted order).
P2a_solver_subsample_first_20.quotient_recomputed_on_subsample: Equivalence relation p ~ p' is over the subsampled solvers. Two probes equivalent on S_full may not be equivalent on S_sub_2a, and vice versa. T_P2a is computed by the equivalence relation restricted to S_sub_2a.

P2b_solver_subsample_last_20.description: Last 20 solvers of LC322 (indices 10..29 in sorted order).

P3a_through_P3f_excluded.rationale: P3 perturbations knock out non-family-3 probes. On the family-3 restricted quotient, the probe set is fixed at family 3; knocking out non-family-3 probes does not change the quotient. The P3 perturbations are no-ops on T_P.
P3a_through_P3f_excluded.P3c_specific_note: P3c knocks out the family under test, zeroing out T_P. Recording P3c as a 'collapse' would be a definitional artifact, not a finding.

P4_excluded.rationale: C-7 is on LC322 only. The LC45 cross-population test was C-5's P4 and is not extended to C-7.

success_criterion_verbatim: Delta(M_C, M_{B1}; T_P) > 0 AND stable under the C-5 perturbation battery restricted to `large_amount_stress`.

degeneracy_on_unperturbed_quotient.applies_to: Delta(M_C, M_{B1}; T_P0), the unperturbed condition
degeneracy_on_unperturbed_quotient.claim: On the unperturbed family-3 quotient T_P0, the condition Delta(M_C, M_{B1}; T_P0) > 0 is an arithmetic identity, not a measurement. The condition holds by construction whenever any solver has a family-3 failure; no application of the estimators is required to verify it.
degeneracy_on_unperturbed_quotient.mechanism: C_genuine accepts a solver if (a) observed failures = 0, or (b) all observed failures share one probe_family. On T_P0, every abstract probe has probe_family = 'large_amount_stress' by inheritance. The set of failures sharing one probe_family is the set of all failures. Condition (b) is vacuously satisfied for any solver with one or more failures. C_genuine accepts every solver with 0 failures (matching B1) AND every solver with one or more failures (differing from B1).
degeneracy_on_unperturbed_quotient.consequence_for_Delta: Delta(M_C, M_{B1}; T_P0) equals the count of solvers with one or more family-3 failures. This is a count, not a measurement of estimator behavior on the quotient.
degeneracy_on_unperturbed_quotient.consequence_for_gap: The unperturbed gap(M_C, M_{B1}; T_P0) is informative. It is not an arithmetic identity; it depends on the specific LC322 data. The unperturbed condition is necessary but uninformative for Delta; the gap is informative.
degeneracy_on_unperturbed_quotient.informative_condition: The informative condition is the stability of the divergence under the four perturbations in the C-7 battery. A 'positive' C-7 result requires the divergence to hold at all 9 lambda values on all 4 perturbations. A 'positive' verdict on the unperturbed Delta alone is not a positive verdict on RQ-C7.

per_perturbation_stability.stability_criterion: A condition C is 'stable' for C-7 if: (a) Delta(M_C, M_{B1}; T_P_C) > 0 at all 9 lambda values in the C-1 sweep, AND (b) the aggregate utility gap on T_P_C exceeds 0.10 at all 9 lambda values.
per_perturbation_stability.collapse_criterion: If any lambda on any condition has Delta <= 0 or gap <= 0.10, the condition is a 'collapse'.
per_perturbation_stability.rq_c7_verdict_logic: A C-7 result with one or more collapses is a 'negative' or 'mixed' result on RQ-C7, not a 'positive' result, regardless of the unperturbed Delta. The success criterion is satisfied only if all 5 conditions are stable.

per_condition.STABLE: Delta > 0 AND gap > 0.10 at all 9 lambda values
per_condition.COLLAPSE: any lambda has Delta <= 0 or gap <= 0.10
per_condition.DEGENERATE: rule or B1 collapses to all-ACCEPT or all-REJECT at a given lambda; record and exclude at that lambda; degenerate on all lambdas -> COLLAPSE

rq_c7.POSITIVE: all 5 conditions are STABLE on LC322. The C-4 gain survives quotient collapse by solver-response equivalence on the family-3 probe set.
rq_c7.NEGATIVE: P0_unperturbed is a COLLAPSE (gap <= 0.10 at any lambda on the unperturbed quotient). The C-4 gain does not survive quotient collapse on the unperturbed family-3 probe set. The verdict is NEGATIVE regardless of perturbation stability; P0 is one of the 5 conditions and the success criterion is the conjunction of all 5 conditions being STABLE.
rq_c7.MIXED: P0_unperturbed is STABLE but one or more of {P1, P2a, P2b, P2c} is a COLLAPSE. The C-4 gain survives quotient collapse on the unperturbed family-3 probe set but not on the perturbed family-3 probe set. The verdict is MIXED, with per-condition status reported.

falsification_criterion.no_aggregation_across_conditions: C-7 verdict is per-condition; the RQ-C7 verdict is one of {POSITIVE, NEGATIVE, MIXED} derived from per-condition status; no other aggregation
falsification_criterion.epistemological_constraint: A POSITIVE C-7 result does not establish that the signal is solver-internal. The H1/H2 ambiguity is not resolved by this phase (per the spec).

aggregate_consistency_check.check: Re-run B1 on unperturbed LC322 (full 30-probe set, not restricted to family-3); recomputed (WA, WR) must equal stored (WA, WR) in data/midweather_fingerprint_lc322.json
aggregate_consistency_check.on_failure: STOP IMMEDIATELY. Do not record any result. Surface discrepancy to Foued.

hard_stop_conditions: Re-run produces (WA, WR) inconsistent with stored aggregates for B1 -> STOP
C-5 perturbation battery parameters are modified after this freeze is committed -> STOP, surface to Foued
C-4 C_genuine decision rule is modified after this freeze is committed -> STOP, surface to Foued
A new estimator is introduced without Foued override -> STOP, surface to Foued
The oracle is varied without Foued override -> STOP, surface to Foued
The quotient construction is changed after this freeze is committed -> STOP, surface to Foued
epistemological_constraints: no negative result inflation beyond tested population (LC322), tested family (large_amount_stress), and tested perturbation set (P1, P2a-c)
no causal language: no because, therefore, explains, caused by, due to
no H1/H2 resolution claim on POSITIVE verdict
